ucs.title: [LLM] BlackTech (Palmerworm) DLL sideloading via legitimate signed binaries in user-writable paths (Waterbear / BendyBear / Flagpro / PLEAD patter
ucs.description: Hunts BlackTech's signature execution pattern: dropping a legitimate, often code-signed third-party binary (e.g., AcroRd32, gup.exe/Notepad++, wsc_proxy.exe/Avast, oleview.exe, javaw.exe) into a user-writable directory alongside a sibling unsigned DLL that gets sideloaded. This chain has been observed across BlackTech's bespoke implants (Waterbear, BendyBear, Flagpro, PLEAD/TSCookie) and is one of the few techniques the group uses with near-uniform consistency across campaigns from 2017–2024.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("AcroRd32.exe","gup.exe","wsc_proxy.exe","oleview.exe","javaw.exe","mscorsvw.exe","GoogleUpdate.exe","vftrace.exe") AND NOT Processes.process_path IN ("*\\Program Files\\*","*\\Program Files (x86)\\*","C:\\Windows\\*","*\\Microsoft\\Edge\\*") by host Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.process_path Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_id
| `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`
| rex field=process_path "(?<exec_dir>.*\\\\)[^\\\\]+$"
| join type=inner dest exec_dir [
    | tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.file_name="*.dll" by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.file_path Filesystem.file_name Filesystem.file_hash
    | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)`
    | rex field=file_path "(?<exec_dir>.*\\\\)[^\\\\]+$"
    | search exec_dir IN ("*\\AppData\\*","*\\ProgramData\\*","*\\Users\\Public\\*","*\\Temp\\*","*\\PerfLogs\\*")
]
| stats values(file_name) as sideloaded_dlls values(process_name) as host_binary values(process_path) as host_binary_path values(parent_process_name) as parent count by dest user
| where mvcount(sideloaded_dlls) >= 1
| `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
let suspectHosts = dynamic(["AcroRd32.exe","gup.exe","wsc_proxy.exe","oleview.exe","javaw.exe","mscorsvw.exe","GoogleUpdate.exe","vftrace.exe","hkcmd.exe","VTTrace.exe"]);
let userPaths = dynamic([@"\appdata\",@"\programdata\",@"\users\public\",@"\temp\",@"\perflogs\"]);
DeviceImageLoadEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (suspectHosts)
| extend lowerInitPath = tolower(InitiatingProcessFolderPath), lowerDllPath = tolower(FolderPath)
| where lowerInitPath has_any (userPaths)
| extend hostDir = tolower(strcat(InitiatingProcessFolderPath, "\\"))
| where lowerDllPath startswith tolower(InitiatingProcessFolderPath)
| join kind=leftouter (
    DeviceFileCertificateInfo
    | project SHA1, IsSigned, IsTrusted, Signer
) on $left.SHA1 == $right.SHA1
| where IsTrusted != true or isempty(IsTrusted)
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, InitiatingProcessAccountName, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessFolderPath, InitiatingProcessParentFileName, FileName, FolderPath, SHA256, Signer, IsSigned, IsTrusted
| join kind=leftouter (
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("rundll32","regsvr32") or InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("winword.exe","excel.exe","outlook.exe","7zG.exe","WinRAR.exe")
    | project DeviceName, InitiatingProcessFileName2=InitiatingProcessFileName, ProcessCreationTime=Timestamp
) on DeviceName
| summarize hostBinaryHits=count(), dllSet=make_set(FileName, 25), parentSet=make_set(InitiatingProcessParentFileName, 10) by DeviceName, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessFolderPath
ucs.rationale: BlackTech is one of the most prolific abusers of DLL search-order hijacking among Chinese-nexus crews; Trend Micro, JPCERT and Symantec all document the same staging pattern: a benign, often signed host EXE planted in AppData/ProgramData with a sibling rogue DLL that loads Waterbear/BendyBear/Flagpro shellcode. The combination of (a) one of a small set of frequently-abused host binaries, (b) execu

ucs.title: [LLM] BlackTech edge-router-to-HQ SSH pivot (CISA AA23-270A 'modified Cisco IOS firmware' tradecraft)
ucs.description: Hunts the lateral-movement leg of BlackTech's signature campaign documented by CISA/NSA/FBI/JPCERT (AA23-270A, Sep 2023): after implanting modified Cisco IOS firmware on a subsidiary's edge router, the actor pivots inbound from the router's management interface over SSH/Telnet into the parent organization's internal servers (DCs, file servers, jump boxes). Detects internal SSH/Telnet sessions originating from network-edge device IPs to crown-jewel hosts.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=true count values(All_Traffic.bytes_in) as bytes_in values(All_Traffic.bytes_out) as bytes_out min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where All_Traffic.dest_port IN (22,23) AND All_Traffic.src_category="network_device" AND All_Traffic.dest_category IN ("domain_controller","file_server","jump_host","crown_jewel") by All_Traffic.src All_Traffic.src_category All_Traffic.dest All_Traffic.dest_category All_Traffic.dest_port All_Traffic.action
| `drop_dm_object_name(All_Traffic)`
| where action="allowed"
| join type=outer dest [
    | tstats summariesonly=true count as auth_count values(Authentication.user) as users from datamodel=Authentication where Authentication.app IN ("sshd","ssh","openssh") AND Authentication.action="success" by Authentication.dest Authentication.src
    | `drop_dm_object_name(Authentication)`
    | rename src as auth_src dest as dest
]
| eval pivot_indicator=if(isnotnull(auth_count) AND auth_count>0, "router_to_internal_ssh_success", "router_to_internal_ssh_attempt")
| table firstTime lastTime src src_category dest dest_category dest_port bytes_in bytes_out users pivot_indicator
| `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
let edgeRouterIPs = dynamic(["10.255.0.1","10.255.0.2"]);  // populate with org's edge/branch router mgmt IPs
let crownJewelTags = dynamic(["DomainController","FileServer","JumpHost","PKI","ExchangeServer"]);
DeviceNetworkEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where ActionType in ("InboundConnectionAccepted","ConnectionSuccess")
| where RemotePort in (22, 23) or LocalPort in (22, 23)
| where RemoteIP in (edgeRouterIPs)
| join kind=leftouter (
    DeviceInfo
    | summarize arg_max(Timestamp, *) by DeviceId
    | project DeviceId, DeviceName, MachineGroup, OnboardingStatus
) on DeviceId
| where MachineGroup has_any (crownJewelTags) or DeviceName has_any ("dc0","-dc-","sql","file","jump","adfs","exch")
| extend pivotKey = strcat(RemoteIP, "->", DeviceName)
| join kind=leftouter (
    IdentityLogonEvents
    | where LogonType in ("Network","RemoteInteractive","Interactive") and Protocol in~ ("SSH","Kerberos","NTLM")
    | where ActionType == "LogonSuccess"
    | project LogonTime=Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, AccountDomain, LogonType, IPAddress
) on DeviceName
| where IPAddress == RemoteIP or isempty(IPAddress)
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, RemoteIP, RemotePort, LocalPort, ActionType, AccountName, AccountDomain, LogonType, InitiatingProcessFileName
| summarize sessions=count(), accounts=make_set(AccountName, 25), firstSeen=min(Timestamp), lastSeen=max(Timestamp) by DeviceName, RemoteIP
| where sessions >= 1
ucs.rationale: This pattern is uniquely BlackTech: the joint CISA/NSA/FBI/JPCERT/NPA advisory AA23-270A specifically called out the actor's practice of compromising a subsidiary's Cisco IOS edge router, installing a backdoored firmware image, and using it as a foothold to SSH inbound to the parent organization's internal infrastructure — abusing the implicit trust between branch/HQ networks.
